vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-714: clean up stale .claude/skills/ references and add unit tests for doneCommand/approveCommand

plan.title: fix(pan-705 followup): clean up stale .claude/skills/ references and add unit tests for doneCommand/approveCommand
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: PAN-705 command taxonomy reorg (pan work list → pan issues, pan cloister/specialists → pan admin *) left project-level .claude/skills/ with stale command refs, 5 orphan skills, 56 missing new skills, and structural bugs (nested dirs). Separately, work/done.ts (535 lines) and work/approve.ts (214 lines) have zero unit tests despite complex pre-flight logic, tracker API paths, and issue-id normalization.
narratives.Proposal: Regenerate .claude/skills/ as an exact mirror of canonical skills/, then extend pan sync to auto-mirror project-level skills on future runs so drift cannot recur. Extract pre-flight checks from doneCommand into src/lib/work/done-preflight.ts and export the existing approve.ts helpers, unit-test both at the helper and command-behavior level following the reopen.test.ts convention.

items.title: Regenerate .claude/skills/ as exact mirror of canonical skills/

narrative.Action: Delete all tracked entries under .claude/skills/ except the .gitignore, then copy every directory from skills/ into .claude/skills/ verbatim. This removes the 5 orphan skills (hume-evi, pan-dashboard-restart, rebase-and-submit, test-specialist-workflow, update-panopticon-docs), fixes structural bugs (nested pan-new-project/pan-new-project/, work-complete/work-complete/), eliminates all stale command references (pan work list, pan cloister start, pan specialists wake), and adds the 56 canonical skills missing from the project tree.

subItems.title: `rg -n "pan work list|pan cloister start|pan specialists wake" .claude/skills/` returns zero matches

Every directory in skills/ has a corresponding directory in .claude/skills/ with byte-identical SKILL.md contents (verify via `diff -rq skills/ .claude/skills/ | grep -v .gitignore` showing no differences)
.claude/skills/.gitignore is preserved unchanged (protects future Panopticon-managed symlinks)
No nested-duplicate directories remain (no `*/pan-new-project/pan-new-project`, no `*/work-complete/work-complete`)
Orphan skills hume-evi, pan-dashboard-restart, rebase-and-submit, test-specialist-workflow, update-panopticon-docs are deleted from .claude/skills/

items.title: Extend pan sync to auto-mirror project-level .claude/skills/ from local skills/

narrative.Action: In src/lib/sync.ts (or a helper called from it), after the existing user-level sync, detect whether the current working directory contains a top-level `skills/` tree with one or more SKILL.md files. If so, mirror skills/* into .claude/skills/* — creating missing directories, updating out-of-date SKILL.md files, and removing directories in .claude/skills/ that no longer exist in skills/. Preserve .claude/skills/.gitignore untouched. Report the count of added/updated/removed skills in the sync output. This must be a no-op for any project without a top-level skills/ directory (i.e., every non-panopticon-cli project).

subItems.title: Running `pan sync` inside panopticon-cli on a clean tree (after regen-claude-skills) produces no changes to .claude/skills/ and reports 0 added / 0 updated / 0 removed
After touching one file under skills/ (e.g., adding a new line to skills/pan-help/SKILL.md), `pan sync` updates the corresponding .claude/skills/pan-help/SKILL.md to match and reports `1 updated`
After deleting a directory under skills/, `pan sync` removes the corresponding directory under .claude/skills/ and reports `1 removed`
Running `pan sync` inside a project without a top-level skills/ directory is a no-op for .claude/skills/ (the existing user-level sync behavior is unchanged)
The existing .claude/skills/.gitignore file is never deleted, overwritten, or moved by the mirror logic
Unit test in tests/lib/sync.test.ts (or similar) exercises the three cases above (no-op, update, remove) against a tmp-dir fixture

items.title: Extract done.ts pre-flight checks into src/lib/work/done-preflight.ts

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/work/done-preflight.ts exporting `checkOpenBeads(workspacePath, issueId)`, `checkUncommittedChanges(workspacePath)`, `checkVBriefACStatus(workspacePath)`, and a composed `runPreflightChecks(workspacePath, issueId)` that returns a string[] of failure lines (empty array = pass). The composed function must also invoke the existing `syncBeadStatusToVBrief` pass over closed beads before running checkVBriefACStatus so the AC check sees the latest state. Refactor doneCommand in src/cli/commands/work/done.ts to call `runPreflightChecks` and, on failure, print the same output and exit(1) — the surface behavior must be byte-identical to the current implementation.

subItems.title: src/lib/work/done-preflight.ts exists and exports checkOpenBeads, checkUncommittedChanges, checkVBriefACStatus, runPreflightChecks
doneCommand in src/cli/commands/work/done.ts no longer contains inline bd/git/AC-check logic; it calls runPreflightChecks and handles the returned failure lines

Running `pan work done <issue>` against a workspace with known failures (open bead + uncommitted file + pending AC) produces the same stderr output as before the refactor (verify by comparing against a pre-refactor capture)
npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass

narrative.Action: Create tests/lib/work/done-preflight.beads.test.ts. Mock execAsync or use a fake workspace with a scripted bd binary. Cover: (1) zero open beads → returns []; (2) N open beads → returns N+1 lines starting with `  Open beads (N):`; (3) bd not installed → returns [] (silent skip); (4) issue-id is lowercased correctly in the bd query.

subItems.title: Test file exists at tests/lib/work/done-preflight.beads.test.ts and imports from src/lib/work/done-preflight.ts
Covers happy path (0 beads), failure path (N beads), bd-missing path, and issue-id casing
All tests pass under `npm test`

narrative.Action: Create tests/lib/work/done-preflight.git.test.ts. Build tmp-dir fixtures via mkdtempSync that simulate (a) monorepo — single top-level .git marker, clean and dirty states; (b) polyrepo — multiple subdirs each with their own .git marker, mixed clean/dirty. Use real `git init` + `git add` where feasible, or mock execAsync to return canned `git status --porcelain` output. Assert failure-line format: `  Uncommitted changes:` for monorepo, `  Uncommitted changes in <subdir>/:` for polyrepo.

subItems.title: Covers monorepo-clean (returns []), monorepo-dirty (returns failure lines), polyrepo-mixed (returns failure lines scoped per subdir)
Covers polyrepo detection fallback: workspace with no top-level .git correctly walks subdirectories
Dotfile subdirectories (e.g., .planning/) are skipped during polyrepo walk
All tests pass under `npm test`

narrative.Action: Create tests/lib/work/done-preflight.ac.test.ts. Build a tmp-dir workspace with a synthetic .planning/plan.vbrief.json containing items with mixed AC statuses. Cover: (1) all AC completed → returns []; (2) one pending AC → returns two lines (header + `  - [ ] <title>`); (3) multiple items with pending AC → returns grouped output; (4) no vBRIEF file present → returns [] (silent skip, matching current behavior).

subItems.title: Covers all-completed, one-pending, multiple-pending, and no-vBRIEF cases
Test fixtures are created and torn down per-test (no leftover tmp dirs)
All tests pass under `npm test`

narrative.Action: Add `export` keywords to findPRForBranch, mergePR, and updateLinearStatus in src/cli/commands/work/approve.ts (checkGhCli already trivially testable). Create tests/cli/commands/work/approve.test.ts following the reopen.test.ts dynamic-import pattern. Cover: (1) findPRForBranch — mocked `gh pr list --json` output returns PR object; empty output returns null; gh failure returns null. (2) mergePR — success returns {success:true}; non-zero exit returns {success:false,error}. (3) updateLinearStatus — mock @linear/sdk; happy path returns true; team-missing returns false; target-state-missing returns false. Use vi.mock or monkey-patch imports as existing tests do.

subItems.title: findPRForBranch, mergePR, updateLinearStatus are exported from src/cli/commands/work/approve.ts
tests/cli/commands/work/approve.test.ts covers happy and failure paths for each of the three helpers
All tests pass under `npm test`

items.title: Command-level tests: doneCommand behavior (force, shadow, id normalization)

narrative.Action: Create tests/cli/commands/work/done.test.ts. Mock the lib-level dependencies (runPreflightChecks, updateLinearToInReview, updateGitHubToInReview, createReviewArtifactsForIssue, saveAgentState, the dashboard auto-review HTTP calls) so the test exercises the *control flow* in doneCommand without hitting real APIs. Cover: (1) --force bypasses runPreflightChecks entirely (spy asserts it is never called); (2) shadow mode (via shouldSkipTrackerUpdate returning true) skips both Linear and GitHub update paths and calls updateShadowState instead; (3) issue-id normalization: `agent-pan-714`, `pan-714`, `PAN-714` all route to the same canonical `PAN-714` / `agent-pan-714` pair; (4) GitHub branch taken when issueId.startsWith('PAN-'), Linear branch otherwise.

subItems.title: --force flag skips pre-flight checks (runPreflightChecks spy asserts zero calls)
Shadow mode routes to updateShadowState and skips both Linear and GitHub tracker update paths
Issue-id normalization covered for agent-prefixed, lowercase, and uppercase inputs
PAN- prefix routes to GitHub branch; non-PAN routes to Linear branch
All tests pass under `npm test` and do not make real network calls

narrative.Action: In tests/cli/commands/work/approve.test.ts (same file as export-test-approve-helpers), add a describe block for approveCommand covering: (1) agent not found → exits with error; (2) PR not found in workspace → exits with error; (3) happy path (PR found, merge succeeds, Linear update succeeds) exits 0 and marks agent state stopped; (4) shouldSkipTrackerUpdate=true skips Linear update but still merges PR. Mock getAgentState/saveAgentState, findPRForBranch, mergePR, updateLinearStatus.

subItems.title: Covers agent-not-found, pr-not-found, happy-path, and shadow-mode-skip
All tests pass under `npm test` and do not make real network or gh CLI calls
